scenario: akm-task adapter validate(component, changes, ctx) → Diagnostic[]. One conformant task (→[]) and one invalid-task-yaml violation (two targets). Mirrors lint/all-types.json's perType shape. SPECIFICATION golden.
adapterStatus: NOT IMPLEMENTED — spec-authored target (future Chunk-2 format-adapter work item). The TaskLinter exists (src/commands/lint/*, dispatched for `tasks` in the frozen all-types lint golden); the adapter wrapper does not.
specSource: docs/architecture/specs/akm-0.9.0-bundle-adapter-spec.md §6 (task validation column: 'invalid-task-yaml: schedule+enabled+one target'), §2/§5.1 (Diagnostic {file, issue, detail, fixed}).
realWorldSource: AKM-native; the task schema (schedule, enabled, one of prompt|command) — src/tasks/, and the frozen all-types task fixture
notes: SPECIFICATION golden. The invalid-task-yaml rule (spec §6) requires a schedule, an enabled flag, and EXACTLY ONE target (prompt XOR command). two-targets.yml declares both a prompt and a command → violation.
issue-code `invalid-task-yaml` is the spec-named code; the `detail` string is illustrative.

conformant.note: schedule '@daily' + enabled false + exactly one target (prompt) → valid.

issues.detail: a task must declare exactly one target (prompt OR command); 'two-targets' declares both a prompt and a command (spec §6 task validation).

invalidTwoTargets.note: single violation: two targets. schedule + enabled are present, so only the one-target rule fails.
